skill.name: deploying-to-production
skill.description: Guides agents through building, publishing, deploying, and verifying a web project with GitHub and Vercel.
skill.summary: Deploy a web project to production through GitHub and Vercel.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged one external command and one weak cryptography pattern. The cryptography finding is a false positive because SKILL.md line 3 is frontmatter description text, while the build command on line 22 is expected deployment behavior with ordinary script execution risk.

low_findings.title: External Build Command Requires Project Trust
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 22 instructs the agent to run npm run build. This is standard for deployment workflows, but it can execute scripts from the target project, so users should review project dependencies and scripts before running it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly present and can run project-defined build scripts. The surrounding deployment workflow gives it legitimate intent, so the risk is low rather than suspicious.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static finding points to SKILL.md line 3, which is a natural language description in frontmatter. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, encryption code, or credential handling appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only descriptive text about GitHub and Vercel deployment. There is no evidence of weak cryptography or security-sensitive code in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Project Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes npm run build, which may execute package scripts from the project being deployed. This is expected for web deployment but should be run only in trusted project contexts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present and semantically relevant to deployment. No malicious command chaining, obfuscation, or exfiltration behavior was found.

content.user_title: Deploy Web Projects to GitHub and Vercel
content.value_statement: Production deployment often fails when build, repository, and hosting steps are handled out of order. This skill provides a clear GitHub and Vercel release checklist.
actual_capabilities: Runs the project build before deployment decisions.
Guides creation or reuse of a GitHub repository.
Directs pushing the project to the default Git branch.
Guides deployment from GitHub to Vercel.
Requires capturing and verifying the live deployment URL.
limitations: Does not include provider-specific authentication setup.
Does not manage secrets, environment variables, or domains.
Does not define rollback or incident response steps.
Requires a project that can build successfully before deployment.

use_cases.title: Launch a Client Website
use_cases.description: Follow a repeatable checklist to build, publish, deploy, and verify a finished web project.

use_cases.title: Ship a Prototype
use_cases.description: Move a working app from local development to a Vercel URL for stakeholder review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Release Steps
use_cases.description: Use the same deployment order across small web projects that use GitHub and Vercel.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy My App
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy this web app to production using the deployment checklist. Stop and explain any build or repository issue before continuing.

prompt_templates.title: Publish Existing Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Build this project, create or reuse a GitHub repository, push the code, deploy to Vercel, and give me the verified URL.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Clean Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the project for deployment readiness, run the build, publish the expected branch to GitHub, deploy through Vercel, and verify the live site.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Deployment Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production deployment workflow to identify which step failed, fix the issue if possible, rerun the step, and verify the final deployment.

output_examples.input: Deploy this React app to production.
output_examples.output: The build completed, the repository was pushed to GitHub, and the project was deployed to Vercel. The final response includes the verified deployment URL.

output_examples.input: Publish this project but reuse my existing GitHub repository.
output_examples.output: The workflow confirms repository reuse, pushes the expected branch, deploys through Vercel, and reports whether verification succeeded.

output_examples.input: The Vercel deployment failed after push.
output_examples.output: The workflow identifies the failed deployment step, checks the error context, applies a fix when possible, and repeats verification.

best_practices: Review package scripts and dependencies before running the build command.
Confirm repository ownership and visibility before pushing code.
Verify the live URL after deployment instead of relying only on provider status.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy before the local build succeeds.
Do not push secrets or local environment files to GitHub.
Do not skip live verification after Vercel reports success.

faq.question: Does this skill create the application?
faq.answer: No. It helps deploy an existing web project that is ready to build.

faq.question: Does this skill require GitHub?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is designed around publishing the project to a GitHub repository.

faq.question: Does this skill require Vercel?
faq.answer: Yes. The deployment steps specifically target Vercel hosting.

faq.question: Can it fix build errors?
faq.answer: It instructs the agent to read build errors, fix issues, and rerun the build before deployment.

faq.question: Does it manage environment variables?
faq.answer: No. Environment variable setup is not described in the skill and must be handled separately.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a low-risk note. The workflow includes a normal build command that can run project scripts.
